name: Stuffed Breast of Veal
recipeInstructions:
STUFFING:
3 Cups Onions, chopped
1/4 cup Chicken fat (schmaltz)
Preheat the oven to 350 degrees.
Prepare the stuffing first: Saute onions slowly in schmaltz until tender and brown. Add matzo meal, apples, parsley, salt, and pepper. Mix thoroughly. Add enough water to bind the stuffing. Let mixture cool before using (yielding 4 cups for 6 servings). Loosely stuff veal pocket just before cooking. Skewer pocket closed.
Scatter onions, garlic, and carrots in a large, flameproof roasting pan. Place veal on top. Rub with salt and pepper. Add enough water to cover vegetables but not veal. Cover pan loosely with aluminum foil. Roast 2 hours. Remove foil. Cook an additional hour until top is crisp and brown.
Remove veal to cutting board. Transfer vegetables to sieve, skimming fat. Press through sieve back into roasting pan. Cook over high heat, scraping bottom of pan. Place sauce in gravy boat. Slice meat along ribs. Each portion should have bone, stuffing, and crispy top. Yield: 6 servings with one rib per serving.
(Note: The original recipe text contained attribution lines and instructions that were not part of the core recipe itself. These have been removed to adhere strictly to the provided rules.)
